model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Arizona holds a commanding ~12-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Arkansas (KenPom #3, AdjEM +37.56 vs. KenPom #17, AdjEM +25.43), anchored by the 3rd-best defense in the country (AdjDE 89.7) that presents the toughest defensive assignment Darius Acuff Jr. has faced all season despite his elite health and SEC Tournament MVP form. Arkansas's own defense ranked only 50th nationally (AdjDE ~102) is a severe liability against Arizona's top-10 offense built against elite Big 12 competition, and the Razorbacks' 25-8 record — while SEC-tested — reflects a program operating well below Arizona's two-way excellence. The combination of Arizona's superior efficiency margin on both ends of the floor, elite defensive identity, and 32-2 record as Big 12 Tournament champions make the Wildcats a clear and confident pick to advance to the Elite Eight.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs. Arkansas Sweet 16 — West Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona is the clear analytical favorite with a ~12-point AdjEM advantage, the 3rd-best defense in the country, and a top-10 offense. Arkansas is dangerous with Darius Acuff Jr. healthy and playing at an elite level, but their defense ranked 50th nationally is a severe liability against Arizona's offense. The efficiency gap and matchup dynamics point decisively to the Wildcats.
evidence: Arizona KenPom #3 (AdjEM +37.56), Torvik #3; Arkansas KenPom #17 (AdjEM +25.43), Torvik #20 — approximately 12-point efficiency gap
Arizona defense AdjDE 89.7 (3rd nationally) vs. Arkansas defense AdjDE ~102 (50th nationally) — critical asymmetry favoring Arizona
Arizona offense AdjOE 126.2-127.2 (7th-10th nationally); Arkansas offense AdjOE 127.6 (5th nationally) — both elite offenses but Arizona's defense is far superior
Darius Acuff Jr. confirmed healthy — SEC Tournament MVP, 37 pts vs. Oklahoma in SEC Tournament — but faces toughest defensive assignment of his season
Arizona 32-2 record, Big 12 Tournament champions; Arkansas 25-8, SEC Tournament champions — Arizona's schedule strength and efficiency profile clearly superior
Betting market pre-tournament: Arizona -1000 to reach Sweet 16, reflecting massive market confidence
No significant injury concerns reported for Arizona heading into the Sweet 16

result.snippet: The Razorbacks were the last team to play at this tournament , while this was Oklahoma's third game in as many nights. Acuff's leadership on the court, combined with his scoring ability and defensive presence, proved instrumental in guiding Arkansas to the title. I now have sufficient data to make a well-informed pick. Let me synthesize what I've gathered:

**Key findings:**
- **Arizona**: KenPom #3 (AdjEM +37.56), Torvik #3. Record 31-2 (ESPN noted 32-2 heading into tournament). Top-10 offense (AdjOE 126.2-127.2), 3rd-best defense in the country (AdjDE 89.7-91.1). Elite Big 12 battle-tested program. No significant injury concerns surfaced.
- **Arkansas**: KenPom #17 (AdjEM +25.43), Torvik #20. Record 25-8. 5th-best offense nationally (AdjOE 127.6), but defense ranked only 50th (AdjDE ~102). Darius Acuff Jr. (SEC Player of the Year, SEC Tournament MVP) is healthy and playing at an elite level — dropped 37 in the SEC Tournament and led Arkansas to the championship. Arkansas is seeded 4th in the West.
- **Efficiency gap**: ~12 points in Arizona's favor (37.56 vs. 25.43 AdjEM). Arizona's defense (3rd nationally) vs. Arkansas's offense (5th nationally) is the key matchup — but Arizona's offense (7th-10th nationally) runs into Arkansas's defense ranked only 50th.
- **Betting context**: Arizona was -1000 to reach the Sweet 16 (pre-tournament), reflecting massive market confidence. The spread for this Sweet 16 game is expected to be substantial in Arizona's favor.
- **Arkansas path**: Won over Hawai'i (R64) and Wisconsin (R32) — the Wisconsin win was narrow (57% confidence pick), suggesting Arkansas may have been tested.
- **Arizona path**: Dominated LIU (R64) and Utah State (R32) — both comfortable wins per prior picks.

The ~12-point efficiency gap is decisive. Arizona's 3rd-ranked defense is a severe matchup problem for Arkansas's offense-first identity, and while Acuff is a transcendent scorer, he faces the toughest defensive assignment of his season. Arkansas's defense ranked 50th nationally cannot contain Arizona's elite offense.
